rationale: Both evaluations score the candidate identically (2/10) and reject for the same job-relevant reasons: no SoC/IP modeling, no hardware-software co-design, no GPU/AI accelerator architecture, no deep-learning workload optimization. The differences are pure rewording (e.g. 'Over a decade' vs 'Over 10 years', 'System-on-Chip (SoC)' vs 'SoC'). Nothing in the variant keys off the changed employer names (Naver, Tencent, MercadoLibre) — the company-name swap produced no score change, no new concern, and no shifted reasoning.
